templates.auto_goods: Resolves YES if, by December 31, {year}, market administrators judge that AI systems can perform, and are actually being deployed at scale to perform, at least {thr} of goods-and-services economic tasks, weighted by their share of 2026 economic output — consistent with the Full Takeoff Model's frac_tasks_automated_goods definition (tasks where AI/compute-based production has displaced human labor as the dominant input). Because no single public statistic tracks this fraction directly, administrators will base their judgment on mainstream economic analyses, labor-automation and occupational studies, and industry deployment data available at resolution time, disclosing their reasoning. If the {thr} threshold is not clearly reached, or the evidence is genuinely ambiguous, by the deadline, the market resolves NO.
templates.auto_rnd: Resolves YES if, by December 31, {year}, market administrators judge that AI systems can perform, and are actually being deployed at scale to perform, at least {thr} of R&D tasks — the research effort devoted to improving AI hardware and software — weighted by their share of 2026 R&D activity, consistent with the Full Takeoff Model's frac_tasks_automated_rnd definition. Because no single public statistic tracks this fraction directly, administrators will base their judgment on mainstream analyses of AI-driven research automation, lab and industry reporting, and academic studies available at resolution time, disclosing their reasoning. If the {thr} threshold is not clearly reached, or the evidence is genuinely ambiguous, by the deadline, the market resolves NO.
templates.gwp_growth: Resolves YES if the real global economic growth rate realized during calendar year {year} exceeds {thr}/year, as reported by the IMF World Economic Outlook (using its estimate for that year once published, even if labeled preliminary). This is a single-year measure: only growth realized within calendar year {year} itself counts, not any other year and not a running maximum. If the IMF WEO series is discontinued or unavailable at resolution time, administrators will substitute the closest equivalent credible public estimate of global real GDP growth for {year}, such as the World Bank's Global Economic Prospects; resolution occurs once final or near-final annual figures are published, typically 6–18 months after year-end.
templates.gwp_growth_max: Resolves YES if, for any calendar year from the market's creation through {year} inclusive, the real global economic growth rate — as reported by the IMF World Economic Outlook (or, if unavailable, the World Bank's Global Economic Prospects) — has exceeded {thr}/year in that single year. This tracks a running maximum: the market resolves YES as soon as any one qualifying year's confirmed growth clears the threshold, and remains YES even if growth subsequently falls back down, unlike the single-year gwp_growth markets. If the IMF WEO series is discontinued, administrators will substitute the closest equivalent credible public estimate of global real GDP growth for the relevant years. If no year through {year} is confirmed to have cleared the threshold once final figures are published, the market resolves NO.
templates.train_run: Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, {year}, any single AI training run is confirmed to have used more than {thr} of training compute, per Epoch AI's training-compute database (or, where Epoch has not yet published a figure, Epoch's methodology or a comparable independent estimate applied to public information about the run). Only completed or substantially completed training runs count; projected, announced-but-undisclosed, or rumored figures do not qualify until a credible compute estimate exists. If Epoch AI discontinues or stops maintaining this tracking, administrators will rely on the best available public estimate of the largest known training run's compute, using a comparable methodology, and will disclose which source they used.
templates.gwp_compute: Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, {year}, credible industry or academic analyses (e.g., Epoch AI's compute/hardware spending estimates, IMF or World Bank data, or comparable analyst estimates of global AI-relevant capital expenditure) indicate that annual worldwide investment in AI-relevant compute — hardware production and deployment for AI — has reached or exceeded {thr} of that year's gross world product. Because no single authoritative real-time tracker exists for this ratio, administrators will weigh the best available public estimates at resolution time and disclose their reasoning if sources diverge. If no year through {year} is confirmed to have cleared the threshold, the market resolves NO.
templates.hw_ratio: Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, {year}, Epoch AI's hardware price-performance dataset (effective FLOP/s per inflation-adjusted dollar for state-of-the-art AI chips) shows an improvement of at least {thr} relative to the best available value for 2026. This tracks hardware cost-efficiency only, not algorithmic/software efficiency gains (see the separate sw_ratio markets for that). If Epoch AI discontinues this dataset, administrators will substitute an equivalent credible public estimate of AI-chip price-performance trends, applying the same {thr} comparison against the 2026 baseline and disclosing the substitute source.
templates.sw_ratio: Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, {year}, credible algorithmic-efficiency research (e.g., Epoch AI's algorithmic progress estimates, or comparable independent studies measuring how the compute required to reach a fixed AI capability level changes over time) indicates that the training or inference compute needed to achieve a benchmark level of AI capability has fallen by a factor of at least {thr} relative to 2026 methods. This tracks algorithmic/software efficiency only, not hardware price-performance gains (see the separate hw_ratio markets for that). If Epoch AI's estimates are unavailable or discontinued, administrators will use the best available equivalent public research, applying the same {thr} comparison against the 2026 baseline and disclosing the substitute source.
templates.full_auto: Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, {year}, market administrators judge that essentially all goods-and-services economic tasks (100% of tasks weighted by 2026 economic value) can be performed, and are being performed at scale, by AI systems without human labor input — consistent with the Full Takeoff Model's full-automation threshold (frac_tasks_automated_goods reaching 100%). Because no clean public statistic tracks full automation directly, administrators will base this judgment on mainstream economic analyses, labor-market data, and credible reporting on the scope of any remaining human-performed work, available at resolution time. If the evidence is ambiguous or full automation is not clearly reached by the deadline, the market resolves NO.
templates.rampup: Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, {year}, market administrators judge that AI systems have automated at least 20% of goods-and-services economic tasks, weighted by 2026 economic value — consistent with the Full Takeoff Model's frac_tasks_automated_goods first reaching 0.2, marking the onset of the modeled economic ramp-up phase. Because no clean public statistic tracks this fraction directly, administrators will base this judgment on mainstream economic analyses, labor-automation studies, and industry deployment data available at resolution time. If the 20% threshold is not clearly reached by the deadline, the market resolves NO.
templates.full_auto_rnd: Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, {year}, market administrators judge that essentially all AI-relevant R&D tasks (100% of hardware and software research effort, weighted by 2026 activity) can be performed, and are being performed at scale, by AI systems without human researcher input — consistent with the Full Takeoff Model's full-automation threshold (frac_tasks_automated_rnd reaching 100%). Because no clean public statistic tracks full R&D automation directly, administrators will base this judgment on mainstream analyses of AI-driven research automation, lab reporting, and academic studies available at resolution time. If the evidence is ambiguous or full R&D automation is not clearly reached by the deadline, the market resolves NO.
templates.agi: Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, {year}, market administrators judge that the largest completed AI training run (per Epoch AI's training-compute database or an equivalent credible estimate) has reached or exceeded the effective training compute that credible contemporaneous AI-forecasting research (e.g., Epoch AI or comparable compute-requirements analyses) estimates would be needed to train a model capable of fully automating goods-and-services tasks, consistent with the Full Takeoff Model's agi_year definition. This is a training-compute-sufficiency threshold, distinct from whether that capability has actually been built or deployed at scale (see the full_auto markets for deployment). Because the compute-requirement estimate itself is uncertain and may be revised as research progresses, administrators will use their judgment of the best available public estimates at resolution time rather than any single fixed number, and will disclose which estimates they relied on.
templates.rampup_rnd: Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, {year}, market administrators judge that AI systems have automated at least 20% of R&D tasks — the research effort devoted to improving AI hardware and software, weighted by 2026 activity — consistent with the Full Takeoff Model's frac_tasks_automated_rnd first reaching 0.2. Because no clean public statistic tracks this fraction directly, administrators will base this judgment on mainstream analyses of AI-driven research automation, lab reporting, and academic studies available at resolution time. If the 20% threshold is not clearly reached by the deadline, the market resolves NO.

notes: Verified against /Users/kas/ftm/ftm/core/model.py (read-only): frac_tasks_automated_goods/_rnd (lines ~858, ~981) are computed as the fraction of tasks where compute-based task input exceeds labor-based task input by >10x, i.e. an economically-weighted 'AI dominates production' criterion — this is why judgment-heavy families describe it as capability-plus-actual-deployment rather than capability alone. biggest_training_run (line ~727) = compute * frac_compute_training, and Epoch's training-compute database is the real-world analog used in ftm/calibration/observed.py (column 'Training compute (FLOP)'). hardware_performance is calibrated in observed.py as 'effective FLOP/year per 2023 USD' from AI-chip production data — i.e. price-performance, sourced from Epoch/industry chip data, distinct from software (algorithmic) efficiency, which the codebase does not calibrate from a named public dataset itself but for which Epoch AI publishes algorithmic-progress estimates in the real world; I cited Epoch's algorithmic-efficiency research for sw_ratio on that basis. frac_gwp_compute (compute_investment / gwp) underlies gwp_compute. agi_year (line ~1295) is defined via frac_automatable_tasks_goods_no_tradeoff reaching 1, i.e. the largest training run's compute meeting the (highly uncertain, model-internal) full_automation_requirements_training parameter — NOT the same as frac_tasks_automated_goods reaching 100% (that's full_auto); agi is a compute-sufficiency/capability threshold that can be met before actual deployment, which the agi template calls out explicitly. rampup/rampup_rnd use the 20%-automation crossing (matches FTM's 'automation_gns_20%'/'automation_rnd_20%' timeline metrics), not the model's internal rampup_trigger parameter (a separate, smaller default trigger not exposed publicly) — the prompt's family description of '20% automation' was followed exactly since that is the publicly meaningful anchor. gwp_growth is a strict single calendar year (matches FTM's log-growth computation over a 1-year window); gwp_growth_max is the running max across years, so it never reverts once triggered — all four thr placeholders for gwp_growth/_max and gwp_compute/auto_goods/auto_rnd/train_run are assumed to already embed their unit/percent sign (e.g. thr='30%', thr='1e28 FLOP'), whereas hw_ratio/sw_ratio thr is assumed to be a bare number with the template supplying the literal 'x' suffix ('{thr}x'), mirroring the exact adjacency shown in the family title templates given in the task. If the integrator's thr formatting convention differs (e.g. train_run thr excludes the FLOP unit), the train_run/gwp_compute/gwp_growth sentences may need a unit word inserted — flagging this as the one substitution-format assumption that should be double-checked before bulk-generating all 486 markets.
